title: Emit fully-indexed events using log4 with zero data length — no memory allocation needed
description: When all event parameters are indexed, there is no ABI-encoded data to write. log4(0x00, 0x00, sig, topic1, topic2, topic3) with zero length reads nothing from memory, emitting only the 4 topics. Solidity's emit always encodes data even when empty.
pattern_before:
// Solidity allocates and encodes empty data
emit DelegateChanged(account, from, to);
pattern_after:
// Zero memory operations — topics only
log4(0x00, 0x00, _DELEGATE_CHANGED_EVENT_SIGNATURE, account, from, to)
when_to_apply: Events where every parameter is indexed (maximum 3 non-signature topics in EVM)
when_not_to_apply: Events with any non-indexed data parameters
